name: Event Sourcing
def: Persisting state as an append-only log of events, deriving current state by replaying them.
problem: Retain a complete, auditable history and reconstruct any past state, not just the latest snapshot.
mechanism: Every change is an immutable event appended to a log; current state is a left-fold over events, often with snapshots to bound replay cost.
benefits: Rebuild state and derive new projections
Natural fit with CQRS and messaging
limits: Replay cost without snapshots; high complexity
failures: Breaking changes to historical event schemas
whenNot: Simple CRUD with no audit/history needs
myths: "Event sourcing = message queue" — it is a system of record, not transport
